tickets.title: §21 — Schema identity: slice 2prime (Schema-as-entity + VersionedSchema); coexistence deferred
tickets.body:
SLICE 2prime LANDED (#96, 2026-07-14): Schema-as-entity + per-version VersionedSchema snapshots are IN SPEC.md. This ticket now tracks ONLY the DEFERRED, DESIGN-STAGE remainder — slice 2b COEXISTENCE (two lenses over one hyperschema; the serving-surface model is unspecified, needs a Myk design pass) and the FUTURE VersionedHyperSchema (Film@termHash, build when §23 needs the gather frozen). Not build-ready; opens at the design stage. --- ORIGINAL BODY BELOW ---

SLICE 2 RE-DECOMPOSED (Myk agreed 2026-07-14) — the plan is now:
SLICE 2prime (BUILDING) = Schema as a first-class entity + per-version VersionedSchema snapshots, CO-LANDED (old slices 2+3 merged — the only §17-non-regressing unit). Lift the resolution Schema out of the registration to a LIVING schema:<name> entity (published via 0.5.0 SCHEMA_SCHEMA / publishSchemaClaims / loadSchema); the registration becomes a binding referencing it; §17's version door (readRegistrationVersions / resolvePinned / REST name@hash) resolves each old version against its FROZEN VersionedSchema snapshot (name@schemaCanonicalHex) — v1 keeps its old reading, exactly as today's inline freezing. SINGLE-LENS for now: Schema.name = hyperschema.name, entity schema:<hyperschema.name>. Ships ONE §20 migration (inline-schema registration → published Schema entity + binding + per-version snapshots; re-sign+negate; composes after slice 1). BREAKING → Myk's merge. See spec/21's clarified 'this snapshot IS §17's per-version freezing' paragraph.
SLICE 2b (DEFERRED — DESIGN-STAGE FIRST) = COEXISTENCE, two lenses over one hyperschema. spec/21 pins the registry key (hyperschema, schema-name) but the SERVING surface is UNSPECIFIED: gql.ts keys the type/query/mutations/hooks dispatch on hyperschema.name; rhizomatic's SchemaRegistry (frozen) refuses duplicate hyperschema names; and what the type is named when schema.name == hyperschema.name (today's universal single-lens case) is unaddressed. Needs a design pass (Myk) on the serving-surface model BEFORE build.
FUTURE (§23-driven) = VersionedHyperSchema (Film@termHash) — the symmetric hyperschema pin, so a fully-reproducible pin is (VersionedHyperSchema, VersionedSchema, roots). PURE LOAM, NO substrate change (termHash + HYPER_SCHEMA_SCHEMA already exist). Latent today; build when §23 needs the gather frozen.

---

SLICE 1 LANDED (2026-07-14, #92): the schema:→hyperschema: entity rename + the immutable-default flip (T1 wave B, now retired) + ONE §20 migration re-signing+negating both. schema: is now free for the Schema entities. REMAINING: slice 2 — the Schema as a first-class entity via SCHEMA_SCHEMA + publishSchemaClaims/loadSchema, named by Schema.name; registration demoted to a binding keyed on (hyperschema, schema-name) so two lenses coexist over one hyperschema. slice 3 — VersionedSchema = name@schemaCanonicalHex, coexisting/never-superseding, pinnable. Each is a PR for Myk (foundational).

---

UNBLOCKED (2026-07-14): the Schema Schema arrived — rhizomatic 0.5.0 ships `SCHEMA_SCHEMA` (#11), `Schema.name/alg`, and `schemaCanonicalHex`. §21 is no longer blocked on a decision; the 0.5.0 adoption LANDED (T8, #89), so §21 is now directly buildable, top of queue, against the real surface — and spec/21 was revalidated to cite the concrete 0.5.0 primitives (SCHEMA_SCHEMA, publishSchemaClaims/loadSchema for Schemas, Schema.name/alg, schemaCanonicalHex). Two of the old ambiguities are resolved by 0.5.0's shape: (1) the Schema Schema lives IN rhizomatic (parity, as filed in #11), not a Loam loam.schema.* fork; (2) the schema-name lives on the Schema itself (`Schema.name`). Remaining §21-native work is unchanged: the Schema as a first-class entity published via SCHEMA_SCHEMA; registration demoted to a binding keyed on (hyperschema, schema-name); VersionedSchema = `name@schemaCanonicalHex`, coexisting/never-superseding; Loam's `schema:`→`hyperschema:` ENTITY-prefix rename shipping its §20 migration; and T1 wave B (the immutable-default flip) folded into the same migration wave. 

---

BLOCKED (2026-07-13) — the §21 implementation hit a substrate wall (found by the build agent's safety valve; nothing was built, no guess shipped).

THE WALL: rhizomatic exports HYPER_SCHEMA_SCHEMA + publishSchemaClaims/loadSchema for the HYPERschema ONLY. There is NO Schema Schema, no at-rest delta vocabulary for the resolution Schema ({props, default}), and the Schema type carries no name — every `schemaName` in the code today is the HYPERschema’s .name. §21’s central move ("a Schema is a domain node with its own deltas, resolved by the Schema Schema, exactly as a Film is") presumes machinery that does not exist in frozen rhizomatic. Building it requires EITHER (a) rhizomatic grows a Schema Schema (a substrate PR + Myk’s call — the frozen-substrate hard limit, kin to the open rhizomatic#10 seam), OR (b) Loam invents a loam.schema.* vocabulary + a hyperschema-over-Schema-deltas. This is a (Myk) + rhizomatic-conversation decision; do NOT guess past it.

PROPOSED DECOMPOSITION (build agent, 2026-07-13): T2a `schema:`→`hyperschema:` prefix rename + §20 migration (most independently shippable; unblocks clean naming; breaking → Myk’s merge). T2b give the resolution Schema a name of its own (prereq for keying/snapshots; blocked on ambiguity 2). T2c key the binding on (hyperschema, schema-name), narrowing the duplicate-name refusal at gateway.ts:708 from "one lens per hyperschema" to "one living lens per (hyperschema, name)" [needs T2a+T2b; breaking]. T2d the Schema as a first-class entity [BLOCKED on the Schema Schema — ambiguity 1, the central wall]. T2e VersionedSchema name@hash snapshots, coexisting + genealogical (derivedFrom, never supersededBy) [needs T2d+T2b; reconcile with §17 registration-versions + the REST name@hash door at rest.ts:218,258]. T2f = T1 wave B: the immutable-default flip — flip assertWritable (gateway.ts:1218) so absent `writable` → nothing writable; every registration gains an explicit writable; migration re-signs old registration deltas adding writable=all-fields to preserve behavior; ALSO fixes a pre-existing bug (genesis.ts:63-68 builds registrationClaims without passing writable). Independent of the ladder but wave-coupled per Myk’s "one migration" note.
ORDER: T2a → {T2b, T2f} → T2c → T2d → T2e. T2d gates full T2c/T2e.

AMBIGUITIES NEEDING MYK (each forces an on-wire guess): (1) the Schema Schema — rhizomatic PR vs loam.schema.*? [gates everything]. (2) where the schema-name lives — on the Schema-at-rest (needs #1) or as Loam-local registration metadata? (3) new hyperschema prefix — recommend `hyperschema:<Name>` (shape-distinguishable); confirm. (4) VersionedSchema specifics — entity-id scheme, short-hash length (example @a1b2c3 = 6 hex — normative?), mint trigger (auto-per-publish vs explicit), the derivedFrom role name, and reconciliation with today’s §17 registration-delta versions + REST name@hash door. (5) wave fragmentation — may T2f (the flip) land its own migration first, or must it ride the final §21 PR? RECOMMENDATION: resolve #1 and #2 first; then T2a and T2f are each clean shippable PRs and T2c/T2d/T2e follow the spine.

--- ORIGINAL BODY (design landed in spec/21-schema-identity.md) BELOW ---

STATUS (2026-07-13): DESIGN LANDED — spec/21-schema-identity.md merged and accepted by Myk; every design question is answered. This ticket is now at the IMPLEMENTATION stage: build the code against the landed design (the design-stage prose below is the settled contract — implement to it, don't re-litigate it). IMPLEMENT: the Schema as a first-class entity with its own id/deltas; registration demoted to a binding keyed on (hyperschema, schema-name); VersionedSchema snapshots (name@short-content-hash) that COEXIST and are independently servable (never supersede — backwards-compat by construction); the schema:-prefix rename shipping its §20 migration (shape-distinguishable). This wave ALSO carries T1 wave B (the immutable-by-default flip) — one migration, one change, every existing registration gaining its explicit writable list. BREAKING on-wire → Myk's merge (P6), never self-merge.

---

DESIGN STAGE — the deliverable is a drafted SPEC §21 (staged as its future spec/21-schema-identity.md file, to land when the work merges) answering all six design questions, argued in a PR. Then STOP and wait for Myk's sign-off in chat before writing implementation code. 'He'd probably approve' is not his word. Lands BEFORE resolvers (§22/T3) and renderers (§23/T4): both need a schema identity that can be named, multiplied, and pinned, and today's registration model gives them none of the three.

WHAT THE PROBE FOUND (facts, with receipts):
- A second lens over a hyperschema REPLACES the first. A registration files under registration:<schemaEntity>, latest wins (registration.ts — registrationEntity), and the registry refuses duplicate hyperschema names, so hyperschema : Schema : roots binds 1:1:1 by construction. There is no way to hold two Schemas over one gather program — the thing the whole HyperSchema/Schema symmetry promises. Not good (Myk).
- The two halves have different standings at rest. The hyperschema definition is a REFERENCE (lives at its own entity, published as schema-schema deltas; the registration merely points at it). The Schema is a CARRIER (inline canonical JSON inside the registration delta). Half the decoupling road is paved; the Schema half never got its own identity.
- Naming conflation residue. The hyperschema definition entity defaults to schema:<Name> (schemaEntityFor), and Loam's prose says 'schema entity' throughout — for what is the HYPERSCHEMA. The 0.3.0 vocabulary pass expunged this at the delta level (rhizomatic.hyperschema.*) but not in Loam's ids and comments. (Rhizomatic's own loadSchema/publishSchemaClaims load and publish HyperSchemas — frozen substrate naming; a conversation in that repo, not ours to fix here.) Renaming Loam's schema: prefix is a breaking on-wire change → ships a §20 migration in the same PR.
- A proto-VersionedSchema already exists. readRegistrationVersions (§17) pins each surviving registration delta — Schema + roots as canonical JSON — by its content address, the version's TRUE NAME. What's missing is everything upstream of it: the version snapshots a REGISTRATION, because a registration is the only identity a Schema has.

THE PROPOSED LADDER (Myk): HyperSchema —many→ Schema —many→ VersionedSchema —many→ API (GraphQL, REST, whatever). A Schema is a LIVING domain node — a view computed from deltas via the Schema Schema, evolving like anything else — until it is snapshotted and REIFIED into a fixed VersionedSchema (plausibly literally the canonical JSON string, content-addressed). Doors serve VersionedSchemas; the living Schema goes on living.

DESIGN QUESTIONS:
1. DECIDED (Myk, 2026-07-12): YES. The Schema becomes a first-class entity — its own id, its own deltas, resolvable like any domain node — with registration demoted to a BINDING (this hyperschema + this schema + these roots, served here). The hyperschema half already works exactly this way; the design stage's job is the shape, not the whether.
2. DECIDED (Myk, 2026-07-12): a DISTINCT snapshot entity, not the registration delta — a version can exist, be named, and be pinned WITHOUT being served, exactly as the snapshot doctrine pulls (see T3). The design pins its shape (plausibly the canonical JSON, content-addressed) and its supersession wiring.
3. OPEN — many-to-many unlocking: the registration:<schemaEntity> keying and the name-unique registry both assume 1:1. A surface type then needs a name of its own, apart from the hyperschema's — what names a lens? (Argue in the draft.)
4. DECIDED (Myk, 2026-07-12): the binding/serving layer owns roots. Roots are a LIVENESS declaration (which entities stay hot), not a scope; they ride the registration-as-binding, never the Schema.
5. The naming pass — expunge the remaining schema/hyperschema blur in Loam's ids and prose; every on-wire rename ships its migration (§20).
6. OPEN — how the upper rungs stand on this: a resolver (§22) rides the Schema and is presumably part of what a VersionedSchema freezes; a renderer (§23) PINS a VersionedSchema — 'renderer pinned to schema vN keeps working forever' needs exactly this ladder under it. (Describe; §22/§23 depend on the answer.)

DEFINITION OF DONE (design stage): a drafted SPEC §21 answering all six questions, argued in a PR, and Myk's sign-off in chat — only then implementation.

CODE ANCHORS: schemaEntityFor (src/gateway/registration.ts:146), readRegistrationVersions (src/gateway/registration.ts:463), and registrationEntity + the duplicate-name refusal in the same file.

TRAPS: the schema: prefix rename (question 5) is on-wire — its migration rides the implementing PR, and the renamed entity ids must be shape-distinguishable from the old form; and rhizomatic's loadSchema/publishSchemaClaims names are frozen substrate — leave them, record the mismatch in prose only.

WAVE NOTE: the §21 implementing PR is the wave that also carries T1's immutable-by-default flip (one migration, one change). 'Lens' is prose, not a type today — question 3 is where the design decides whether it gets one; until then write Schema when you mean the Schema.
